paper_title: Beyond Static Leaderboards: Predictive Validity for the Evaluation of LLM Agents

research_equation.assumption: In-sample aggregate score rank (Ȳ_c) is a sufficient statistic for deployment rank
research_equation.problem: Empirically false: CODS-2025 ρ=-0.13 (execution), ρ=0.69 (planning, upper CI on falsification threshold)
research_equation.after: R(leaderboard) should predict R(deployment) via PV(c) = αȲ_c - βσ_{Y_c,OOD} - γIQR(Y_c)
research_equation.operationalization: Three OOD criteria (A: held-out, B: cross-subset, C: adversarial) + four falsification thresholds

first_pass.synopsis: Position paper arguing that aggregate-score leaderboards underspecify deployed LLM agent evaluation. Proposes predictive validity (PV) as ranking criterion with 12-tier measurement apparatus and three OOD criteria.
first_pass.novelty: First paper to propose PV (correlation of in-sample to out-of-sample rank) as a leaderboard ranking criterion, paired with explicit falsification thresholds
first_pass.audience: AI/ML evaluation methodologists, agent benchmark designers, LLM deployment operators

method_strengths: Explicit falsification thresholds (4 conditions) — rare in evaluation methodology papers
Convergent architectural sensitivity across 14 implementation studies provides structural evidence without requiring new controlled experiments
12-tier apparatus is grounded in existing benchmarks, not invented de novo
Five convergence patterns (Appendix E) distill cross-study insight elegantly
Pre-registered pilot design with clear decision rules (low-power/middle/strong findings)
method_weaknesses: All evidence from one domain (industrial asset O&M on AssetOpsBench) — domain specificity is a real limitation
14 extension studies are unpublished implementation reports, not peer-reviewed
PV score weights (α, β, γ) are not finalized — no empirical fitting done
Tier orthogonality is asserted as a 'working hypothesis' without empirical test
No controlled experiment run by the authors themselves — the paper is a synthesis
key_figures: Figure 1: 14 extensions along 6 axes (visual overview of study scope)
Figure 2: Benchmark × tier coverage (shows no benchmark >5/12 tiers)
Figure 3: Headline improvement per team (log-scale, colored by axis)
Figure 4: Evidence heatmap (14 studies × 4 evaluation dimensions)
Figure 5: Rank correlations with Fisher-z CIs and falsification threshold

hidden_assumptions: Tier orthogonality: the 12 tiers are assumed non-redundant without empirical testing
OOD criterion completeness: three criteria (held-out, cross-subset, adversarial) exhaust the 'deployment-relevant' shift space
PV formula form: linear combination (α-β-γ) is assumed adequate without testing nonlinear forms
One-domain generalizability: AssetOpsBench patterns transfer to other MCP domains
Spearman ρ=0.85 threshold: arbitrary or empirically grounded? The paper does not justify this specific value beyond CODS-2025 data

dimension_collapse.challenge: Aggregate scores hide qualitatively different behavior

judge_reflexivity.evidence_gap: No standardized rule pipeline shared across leaderboards yet

rank_instability.evidence_gap: PV score weights not empirically fit; full study not run

infrastructure_conflation.challenge: MCP-stdio overhead confounded with reasoning ability
infrastructure_conflation.evidence_gap: No persistent MCP server standard exists

scenario_binding.challenge: 141-scenario corpus is binding constraint on extension work

key_concepts.definition: Correlation between in-sample rank and out-of-sample rank, not in-sample mean
key_concepts.significance: Primary contribution: new ranking criterion for agent leaderboards

key_concepts.definition: Leaderboard rankings inconsistent across distributionally-different settings
key_concepts.significance: Core empirical phenomenon motivating the position

key_concepts.definition: Measurement instrument with model-specific biases; leaderboard measures its own judge
Multiple independent teams converging on same measurement gaps from different architectures
key_concepts.significance: Epistemic framing of evidence: not independent replication but convergent sensitivity

key_concepts.definition: Four explicit conditions under which the position is considered refuted
key_concepts.significance: Methodological best practice: makes position falsifiable, not merely persuasive

related_work_clusters.relationship: Source benchmarks whose metrics are consolidated into the 12-tier apparatus
Anchoring critique — leaderboard ranks reflect user utility 'only by accident'
Predecessor frameworks that address multi-dimensionality for single-shot models but not agents

papers: Xuan et al. (2026): Interactive Evaluation Requires a Design Science
related_work_clusters.relationship: Names the structural change without proposing a ranking criterion — gap this paper fills
